calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-07-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-27-2019 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-27-2019 Continued To Mar-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Lam appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Lam stated that he just moved to Boston. The Court stated that having a self represented party administering an estate is always a concern, that probate is very technical and complex, that it involves statutes and rules that when not followed can cause delay and liability, and that there are a lot of required steps that pro pers frequently do not do. Mr. Lam stated that he has been working with a legal document assistant and that person has provided guidance. The Court informed Mr. Lam that the legal document assistant is not a lawyer and that he or she can not give him legal advice, and asked Mr. Lam if he knows what the next step is. Mr. Lam stated that he is the only heir, that his Father had a condominium, that he thinks it is a straight forward process, that he needs authority to work with a licensed realtor to get the property sold and that he needs to open an estate account. The Court informed Mr. Lam that he is required to do an inventory and appraisal within four months of appointment, that it is prepared to order a bond unless he is appointed with limited authority and strongly suggest hiring an Attorney. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Mr. Lam the opportunity to get an Attorney on board.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
